calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-29-2009 Dismissal Or Judgment On File As To All Defendants

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default And Default Judgment Ccp 473.5
Off Calendar Jul-08-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Catherine Delorey, Esq. of McInerney & Dillon, 510-465-7100 for defendant and moving party Shamrock Structures, Inc.
Steven Weinstein, Esq., 510-336-2181 for plaintiff and opposing party U-Save Equipment Rental, Inc.

RULING:
ARGUED and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows --
DEFENDANT SHAMROCK STRUCTURES, INC.'S Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default And Default Judgment Ccp 473.5 DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT PROVED LACK OF ACTUAL NOTICE AND HAS NOT MOVED FOR RELIEF WITHIN REASONABLE TIME FROM KNOWLEDGE THAT THERE WAS A DEFAULT, WHICH IT HAD HAD IN NOVEMBER 2008. Prevailing party to submit form of order.
Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-19-2009 Continued To Jul-22-09 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar May-21-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Steven Weinstein, Esq. appeared for plaintiff U-Save Equipment Rental, Inc., a California corporation.
James Lew, president and one of the owners of U-Save Equipment Rental, Inc.
No appearance by defendant Shamrock Structures, Inc., a California corporation.
Default was entered against defendant on July 1, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed on May 19, 2009.
Dismissals without prejudice were entered against defendants Edward Ong, Margaret Ong, Allen Ong, Warren Robert Ong and Randy Terry Ong on May 18, 2009.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff U-Save Equipment Rental, Inc., a California corporation, and against the Defaulted Defendant Shamrock Structures, Inc., a California corporation as follows:

Damages/Principal - $10,163.92.
Prejudgment Interest - $1,278.80.
Attorney's Fees - $6,765.00.
Costs - $515.77, for a total judgment in the amount of $18,723.49.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Apr-22-2009 Continued To May-21-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 218
